title: mespilus monogyna var. genuina
intro: Mespilus monogyna var. genuina, also known as the wild medlar, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The wild medlar has been traditionally used to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, offering numerous health benefits. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and fruit of Mespilus monogyna var. genuina are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other ailments.
